data.title: ECB keeps interest rate unchanged
data.description:
The European Central Bank decided to keep the three key ECB interest rates unchanged on Thursday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Crypto stocks plunge as rate cut hopes dampen
data.description:
Cryptocurrency stocks were trading lower after the Federal Reserve dampened rate cut expectations with a cautious commentary from Chair Jerome Powell.

The Fed's policymaking arm held its key interest rate at 3.50%-3.75%, as widely expected. The central bank made no changes to its median interest rate projections either, with a quarter-point reduction still penciled in for 2026.

With the FOMC keeping its key rate unchanged, Powell called the level "within plausible estimates of neutral"—the "point at which interest rates neither stimulate nor depress the economy.

There will be some effects on inflation coming forward, he said. But he expects to see more progress on inflation at about mid-year. "If we don't see inflation progress, you won't see a rate cut," Powell noted at his post-rate decision press conference.

Wall Street has stopped believing in an interest-rate cut this year by the Fed. Traders have now wiped out the last fully priced bet for a 2026 rate reduction.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices faced pressure after a brief market correction in the wake of the Fed announcement. BTC-USD slipped below the $70K level on Thursday before the U.S. market open. Ethereum (ETH-USD) was trading 3.32% lower at ~$2,130.

Crypto-linked stocks that were the worst affected include:

  • IREN (IREN) -4.36% to $40.37
  • Strategy (MSTR) -4.25% to $134.59
  • Hut 8 (HUT) -4.27% to $47.25
  • Circle Internet Group (CRCL) -4.16% to $127.31
  • Cipher Digital (CIFR) -3.82% to $14.11
  • Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) -3.77% to $32.41
  • Coinbase Global (COIN) -3.45% to $195.32
  • Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) -3.36% to $20.69
  • Robinhood Markets (HOOD) -3.20% to $72.50
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) -3.14% to $8.64

data.title: Viatris sets long-term targets ahead of investor event
data.description:
Viatris (VTRS) announced its long-term financial targets and capital allocation strategy through 2030 as the Pfizer (PFE) spinoff prepares to hold its investor event with senior executives in attendance.

At the event scheduled to begin at 10:00 a.m. ET, the company expects to detail plans to deliver 5% - 6% total revenue CAGR as well as 7% - 8% in adjusted EBITDA CAGR, and 9% - 10% in adjusted EPS CAGR through 2030.

The long-term goals represent the company's base-case business targets and potential additional drivers. As for the base business, Viatris (VTRS) forecasts a 3% - 4% CAGR in total revenue, a 4% - 5% CAGR in adjusted EBITDA, and a 6% - 7% CAGR in adjusted EPS.

The adjusted EPS CAGR accounts for share buybacks, and the projection for adjusted EBITDA includes operating leverage from cost savings from a recently completed enterprise-wide strategic review, which is expected to generate $650M in gross cost savings over three years.

Viatris (VTRS) projects over $2.7B in annual free cash flow in 2030 and more than $11B in cash available for deployment through 2030 under its base case long-term targets.

data.title: Alibaba stock sinks after results miss, raising doubts on AI ambitions
data.description:
Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) reported wide misses for revenue and profit in the fourth quarter, raising investor concerns about whether the company's investments in artificial intelligence are bearing fruit as it continues to pour resources into it.

Shares of the company are down more than 7% in the U.S. before the opening bell on Thursday.

Revenue from China e-commerce rose 16% to RMB 159.35B but missed the RMB 165.94B consensus estimate polled by Bloomberg.

International digital commerce revenue grew only 3.8% to RMB 39.2B, below the estimate of RMB 41.67B.

Cloud intelligence revenue grew 36% to RMB 43.28B, just slightly above the consensus estimate of RMB 42.36B.

Seeking Alpha analyst Geneva Investor, in immediate reaction to the report, said they remain unconvinced of Alibaba’s AI story.

"AI investments come at the detriment of net income (down 67% YoY). With little to show in return: cloud revenue growth (+36% YoY) is insufficient when Microsoft’s Azure (MSFT) is growing at 39% from a much larger base," the analyst said. "The complex macro situation, including limitations in accessing top-of-the-line microchips, acts as a further headwind for BABA’s AI ambitions."The analyst also expressed worries about the company's quick commerce push as marketing expenses went from 15% to 25% of revenue, risking a lose-lose price war with JD.com (JD) in a weak domestic market.

Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders fell sharply to RMB 16.32B, or RMB 5.93 per share, compared to RMB 48.95B, or RMB 20.39 per share, for the same period last year. 

On an adjusted basis, Hangzhou, China-based Alibaba earned RMB 7.09, below the average analyst estimate of RMB 12.34. 

Total revenue rose only 1.7% to RMB 284.84B and missed the consensus estimate of RMB 289.79B.

data.title: Prediction markets signal 'higher-for-longer' as traders eye possible Fed rate hikes
data.description:
Prediction markets are signaling a shift toward a “higher-for-longer” interest rate outlook following the Federal Reserve’s latest policy decision, with some traders even pricing in the possibility of additional rate hikes later this year.

At its most recent meeting, the Federal Reserve held its benchmark federal funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75%, a move that was widely anticipated. However, remarks from Chair Jerome Powell appear to have reinforced expectations that policy may remain restrictive for an extended period. 

Data from the prediction market Kalshi shows traders increasingly assigning probabilities to higher rates by year-end. Market participants currently see a 53% likelihood that the federal funds rate will exceed 5.25% following the December FOMC meeting, compared to a 32% probability that rates remain above 2.75%. 

The dispersion in forecasts highlights ongoing uncertainty around the path of monetary policy, as investors weigh persistent inflation risks against signs of slowing economic momentum and geopolitical tensions. 

Outlined below is what traders on Kalshi are pricing in when asked, what will the Fed funds rate be after the December meeting? 

  • Above 2.75% — 32% chance
  • Above 3.00% — 73% chance
  • Above 3.25% — 64% chance
  • Above 3.50% — 56% chance
  • Above 3.75% — 48% chance
  • Above 4.00% — 15% chance
  • Above 4.50% — 48% chance
  • Above 4.75% — 59% chance
  • Above 5.00% — 53% chance
  • Above 5.25% — 53% chance 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ). 

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: Humacyte secures $1.48M Symvess purchase commitment in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
• Humacyte (HUMA) on Thursday said it received a minimum purchase commitment of $1.475 million for its Symvess tissue-engineered vessel to support a clinical evaluation and outreach programme in hospitals in Saudi Arabia.
  • The company said the programme will run alongside ongoing negotiations with a Saudi-based entity to establish a joint venture and licensing agreement to commercialize Symvess in the country.
  • Humacyte added it will provide training to medical professionals and has agreed not to pursue other commercialization negotiations in Saudi Arabia until July 2, 2026.
  • The company said Symvess remains investigational for uses beyond its existing U.S. regulatory approval, and finalization of the joint venture and licensing deal remains subject to further negotiations and definitive agreements.
  • HUMA -10.37% premarket to $0.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: S&P 500 nears typical geopolitical 'bottom window' as Iran risks intensify, Deutsche Bank says
data.description:
Global equity markets are entering a phase that has historically marked the worst point of geopolitical-driven selloffs, even as the news flow around Iran deteriorates, according to Deutsche Bank.

The S&P 500 (SP500) is down just under 4% over 13 trading days since the latest strikes on Iran, broadly tracking the average pattern seen across more than 30 geopolitical shocks since 1939, Deutsche Bank analysts said.

Historically, U.S. equities tend to bottom around 15 trading days after such events, with average declines of a little over 4% relative to pre-shock levels. Markets are now approaching that window.

The bank noted that this “average path by day” differs from the maximum drawdown typically seen during such episodes. On that measure, declines are deeper—around 6% on a median basis and roughly 8% on average—reflecting the lowest point reached at any stage rather than on a specific day.

In calendar terms, the trough has often occurred roughly three weeks after the initial shock, suggesting markets are nearing a critical inflection point. Recovery has historically followed within weeks, with median returns back to pre-shock levels by around day 34.

The latest escalation in the Middle East has added to uncertainty. Energy markets have reacted sharply, with reports of significant damage to a major LNG facility in Qatar driving spikes in gas and oil prices.

The key question for investors is whether the worsening backdrop signals a deeper, more prolonged selloff or whether markets are following a familiar geopolitical pattern, Deutsche Bank said.

Here is a chart posted by Deutsche Bank: 

 

S&P ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: Two Harbors gets all-cash proposal, competing with UWM Holdings deal
data.description:
Two Harbors Investment (TWO) stock surged 11% in Thursday premarket trading after it received an unsolicited proposal for $10.70 per share in cash, the company said on Thursday.

That comes after the mortgage REIT had already agreed to be acquired  by mortgage wholesaler UWM Holdings (UWMC) in an all-stock deal that valued each share of TWO at $11.94 on Dec. 16, 2025. At the close of March 18, the UWMC deal valued each share of TWO at ~$8.61.

The new proposal provides for the payment of the $25.4M termination fee that would be required under the UWMC agreement. The unsolicited proposal represents a 12% premium to Two Harbors' (TWO) closing price of $9.53 on March 18.

Two Harbors (TWO) board's ad hoc committee has determined that the unsolicited proposal could "reasonably be expected to lead to a 'company superior proposal'" as defined in the UWMC agreement. The company didn't disclose the identity of the new suitor.

The committee, though, hasn't determined whether the proposal is superior to the UWMC deal, and that agreement remains in effect. The board continues to recommend the UWMC transaction.

If the new proposal leads to a superior proposal, then UWM Holdings (UWMC) will have three business days to negotiate with Two Harbors (TWO) to propose any revisions to its agreement.

UWM Holdings (UWMC) stock gained 2.5% in Thursday premarket.

data.title: U Power prices $6 million U.S. public offering of shares and warrants
data.description:
• U Power Limited (UCAR) (Nasdaq: UCAR) on Thursday said it priced an underwritten public offering of 13.36 million units at $0.449 per unit, aiming to raise about $6.0 million in gross proceeds.
  • Each unit comprises one Class A ordinary share and one warrant exercisable at $0.449 per share, with terms allowing for price adjustments shortly after closing and an option for cashless exercise.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 20, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Maxim Group is acting as the exclusive underwriter and has a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.004 million shares and/or warrants.
  • The securities are being offered under a registration statement declared effective by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on March 18, 2026.
  • UCAR -68.86% premarket to $0.1398.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: RIVN, SNDK, TSLA, MRK
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Thursday, a day after wholesale inflation came in hot and the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Rivian (RIVN) surged 8.28% in premarket trading after Uber announced a $1.25B investment to purchase 10,000 fully autonomous R2 robotaxis, with an option for up to 40,000 more. Uber’s investment will extend through 2031, subject to the achievement of certain autonomous milestones by specific dates.

Sandisk (SNDK) fell 4.81% in premarket trade even as Citi raised its price target on the company following Micron’s results and guidance, which suggested continued strength in the storage market. Analyst Asiya Merchant noted that data center NAND bit demand is accelerating on AI use cases, with roughly 20% industry NAND bit growth expected in calendar year 2026 as demand significantly exceeds available supply.

Tesla (TSLA) slipped 1.58% in premarket trading after federal safety regulators intensified scrutiny of the company’s automated driving system. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said Thursday it is opening an engineering analysis to evaluate Tesla’s Full Self Driving Beta and Full Self Driving (Supervised) degradation detection system, raising concerns about its ability to handle poor roadway conditions.

Merck (MRK) edged down 0.26% in premarket trade after its animal health unit announced that the U.S. FDA approved a label expansion for Bravecto Quantum, its once-yearly parasiticide treatment. The revised label allows use of the veterinarian-administered injection for the treatment and control of Asian longhorned tick and Gulf Coast tick in dogs for 12 months.
